window.label: Entire history
window.phrase: the entire available history up to 2026-07-22

evidence.claim: The UAE military advisory group handed over modernized barracks and training facilities to the Somali National Army on August 10, 2024.
evidence.snippet: UAE military officials transferred newly upgraded facilities to Somali security forces to bolster local counter-terrorism training capabilities.

evidence.claim: DP World completed a modern USD 12 million security and systems upgrade at the Port of Bosaso in Puntland on September 15, 2024.
evidence.snippet: DP World finished installing state-of-the-art security, scanning, and customs clearing systems at Bosaso Port.

evidence.claim: The UAE Red Crescent delivered 2,000 tons of emergency drought relief food supplies to Mogadishu Port on October 24, 2024.
evidence.snippet: A major relief consignment dispatched by the UAE arrived in Mogadishu to support families suffering from severe climate shocks.

evidence.claim: The UAE and Somalia signed a comprehensive security cooperation agreement on December 12, 2024, to expand maritime defense collaboration.
evidence.snippet: The two countries signed a defense accord focusing on joint maritime security, counter-piracy operations, and border control.

evidence.claim: The Abu Dhabi Fund for Development (ADFD) funded a USD 25 million municipal solar grid project in Hargeisa, completed on February 15, 2025.
evidence.snippet: An ADFD-backed solar project went online, generating clean electricity for public infrastructure and major regional hospitals.

evidence.claim: flydubai launched direct commercial passenger services to Garowe, Somalia, starting March 1, 2025, operating thrice-weekly.
evidence.snippet: flydubai expanded its footprint in Somalia, launching regular routes to Garowe International Airport to support regional mobility.

evidence.claim: The UAE Red Crescent completed a major restoration of the Sheikh Zayed Hospital in Mogadishu, reopening it on April 18, 2025.
evidence.snippet: Mogadishu's Sheikh Zayed Hospital resumed operations, offering modern clinical care and diagnostics financed by UAE humanitarian funds.

evidence.claim: DP World signed a preliminary expansion agreement for the Berbera Economic Zone on May 20, 2025, committing USD 50 million.
evidence.snippet: DP World agreed to expand regional logistics capabilities by constructing new dry storage and trade infrastructure in Berbera.

evidence.claim: Somali Prime Minister Hamza Abdi Barre met with UAE leadership in Abu Dhabi on June 12, 2025, to discuss regional security investments.
evidence.snippet: Bilateral talks focused on securing trade corridors, port operations, and strengthening UAE-backed security training initiatives.

evidence.claim: The UAE contributed USD 8 million in emergency funding to the UN World Food Programme's Somalia operations on July 5, 2025.
evidence.snippet: The UAE provided funding to secure therapeutic food distribution for severe climate-affected families across southern Somalia.

edges.claim: The UAE military advisory group handed over modernized barracks and training facilities to the Somali National Army on August 10, 2024.
DP World completed a modern USD 12 million security and systems upgrade at the Port of Bosaso in Puntland on September 15, 2024.
The UAE Red Crescent delivered 2,000 tons of emergency drought relief food supplies to Mogadishu Port on October 24, 2024.
The Abu Dhabi Fund for Development (ADFD) funded a USD 25 million municipal solar grid project in Hargeisa, completed on February 15, 2025.
flydubai launched direct commercial passenger services to Garowe, Somalia, starting March 1, 2025, operating thrice-weekly.
DP World signed a preliminary expansion agreement for the Berbera Economic Zone on May 20, 2025, committing USD 50 million.
